content: We can model Retrieval-Augmented Generation for artificial agents after the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction that governs biological memory retrieval, and gate this retrieval using a Transformer attention mechanism that prioritizes context-relevant episodic memory traces the same way active learning and intrinsic curiosity (ICM) prioritize novel, high-information environmental exploration. This creates a curiosity-driven episodic retrieval system that automatically updates its external knowledge base with high-value new episodic memories accumulated through the agent's interaction with the world.
source_topics: Transformer attention mechanism for episodic memory
